claim_attributions.claim_text: Deterministic readiness: fatigued (score 49.1).
Aim for a consistent 8+ hour sleep opportunity nightly and keep the wind-down routine stable.
Keep weekday sessions flat and easy; do not add hills or extra quality while fatigue is still present.
Fuel any session longer than 75 minutes with normal carbohydrate intake before and after, and recover with a mixed meal within 1-2 hours.
Hydrate on bike and run days, and keep post-session mobility to 10-15 minutes rather than adding heavy strength work this block.
Recent training load is elevated versus baseline and recovery signals are still muted, so unplanned intensity could compound fatigue.
Only one hard session is scheduled in any 7-day window; adding extra tempo, intervals, or hill work would break the guardrails.
Weekend-only trail access compresses specificity, so weekday road and bike work must stay truly easy and flat.
HRV is unavailable in the recent telemetry, which lowers confidence in fine-grained recovery calls.
Absorb the recent long trail load and reset recovery before resuming easy work.
Restore run frequency without adding fatigue.
Add aerobic volume with low musculoskeletal stress.
Keep the aerobic rhythm and maintain run frequency midweek.
Keep load low while supporting recovery and circulation.
Extend continuous aerobic work without crossing into quality.
Maintain long-run trail specificity and climbing economy without adding intensity.
Introduce a second weekend trail stimulus while staying aerobic.
Add aerobic volume with minimal impact between run days.
Keep the run frequency steady without adding fatigue.
Support recovery while building aerobic consistency.
Consolidate the early build and keep fatigue from stacking.
Extend continuous aerobic work while staying far from hard intensity.
Repeat the long trail pattern and reinforce climbing economy.
Maintain trail frequency before the only quality session in the block.
Keep the aerobic rhythm going before quality later in the week.
Absorb the load before the tempo day and protect the legs.
Touch controlled quality without stacking intensity.
Recover from tempo and keep the 7-day hard count at one.
Rebuild volume after the quality day without adding stress.
Maintain long-run trail specificity and endurance while staying aerobic.
Start the recovery/consolidation week and shed accumulated fatigue.
Keep movement going while holding the week in a recovery emphasis.
Maintain run rhythm without adding intensity in the recovery week.
Add a second rest day in the consolidation week so the load drops meaningfully.
Keep aerobic maintenance in the recovery week without adding strain.
Finish the consolidation week with low-impact aerobic work.
Retain trail contact while the block stays in recovery/consolidation mode.

data_notes: Rollup totals were treated as authoritative for the 7-day and 28-day comparisons.
HRV had 0 usable days in the recent 7-day window, so recovery interpretation relies on sleep and resting HR.
Missing values were treated as missing; no -1 values were used in the summary fields above.
Historical profile shows a trail-running background and meaningful prior capacity, but those peaks were not used to justify aggressive near-term volume.
Plan dates start on 2026-03-22 because that is the final day in the provided daily array and therefore the operational 'today' in this run.
Readiness status was set from deterministic readiness_and_risk_forecast.json.
Guardrails: converted ['2026-03-23', '2026-03-25', '2026-03-27'] to rest days to satisfy min_rest_per_7d=1 (rolling window).
Guardrails: enforced ramp rate (max_ramp_pct=0.0%). last7_hours=8.046, allowed_hours=8.046, planned_hours=7.100.

effective_constraints.lifestyle_notes: Only flat road runs or cycling allowed during the week days. Trail runs limited to Saturday or Sunday only.

meta.lifestyle_notes: Only flat road runs or cycling allowed during the week days. Trail runs limited to Saturday or Sunday only.

days.purpose: Absorb the recent long trail load and reset recovery before resuming easy work.
days.title: Recovery rest
days.workout: Warm-up: 10 min easy walk and mobility to reduce stiffness. Main set: full rest from structured training; optional 20-30 min gentle walk only if you feel tight. Cool-down: 5-10 min light stretching and feet-up recovery. Target: RPE 1/10. Terrain/equipment: off feet, no structured session.

days.purpose: Restore run frequency without adding fatigue.
days.title: Easy road run
days.workout: Warm-up: 10 min easy jog plus light dynamic drills to loosen the legs. Main set: 40 min easy flat road run at conversational effort, keeping the pace smooth and relaxed. Cool-down: 10 min easy jog/walk and calf/hip mobility. Target: RPE 2-3/10. Terrain/equipment: flat road only.

days.purpose: Add aerobic volume with low musculoskeletal stress.
days.title: Easy aerobic bike
days.workout: Warm-up: 10 min easy spin to get the legs moving. Main set: 50 min easy aerobic cycling with smooth cadence and light pressure on the pedals. Cool-down: 10 min very easy spin and mobility for hips and calves. Target: RPE 2-3/10. Terrain/equipment: flat road or trainer.

days.purpose: Keep the aerobic rhythm and maintain run frequency midweek.
days.title: Easy road run
days.workout: Warm-up: 10 min easy jog plus drills. Main set: 40 min easy road run, staying conversational and efficient on flat terrain. Cool-down: 10 min easy jog/walk and light mobility. Target: RPE 2-3/10. Terrain/equipment: flat road only. If fatigue remains high, shorten the main set to 30 min and keep it purely easy.

days.purpose: Keep load low while supporting recovery and circulation.
days.title: Easy aerobic bike
days.workout: Warm-up: 10 min easy spin. Main set: 30 min easy aerobic cycling with very smooth cadence and low resistance. Cool-down: 10 min easy spin plus hip and back mobility. Target: RPE 2/10. Terrain/equipment: flat road or trainer.

days.purpose: Extend continuous aerobic work without crossing into quality.
days.title: Controlled aerobic road run
days.workout: Warm-up: 10 min easy jog with relaxed mechanics. Main set: 45 min controlled aerobic road run, a touch firmer than easy but still conversational. Cool-down: 10 min easy jog/walk and mobility. Target: RPE 3-4/10. Terrain/equipment: flat road only.

days.purpose: Maintain long-run trail specificity and climbing economy without adding intensity.
days.target_intensity: RPE 3/10, easy aerobic with hiking on climbs
days.title: Long trail run
days.workout: Warm-up: 15 min easy jog on gentle terrain plus light drills. Main set: 90 min easy trail run on rolling terrain, hiking the steeper climbs and keeping the effort conversational throughout. Cool-down: 15 min easy jog/walk and mobility. Target: RPE 3/10. Terrain/equipment: weekend trail with moderate climbing. If fatigue is still elevated, cut 15-20 min from the main set and keep the whole run easy.

days.purpose: Introduce a second weekend trail stimulus while staying aerobic.
days.title: Easy trail run
days.workout: Warm-up: 10 min easy jog plus mobility. Main set: 55 min easy trail run on smooth weekend terrain, keeping the effort conversational and the descents controlled. Cool-down: 10 min walk/jog and mobility. Target: RPE 2-3/10. Terrain/equipment: weekend trail, light climbing only.

days.purpose: Add aerobic volume with minimal impact between run days.
days.title: Easy aerobic bike
days.workout: Warm-up: 10 min easy spin. Main set: 40 min easy aerobic ride with relaxed cadence and no surges. Cool-down: 10 min very easy spin and mobility. Target: RPE 2-3/10. Terrain/equipment: flat road or trainer.

days.purpose: Keep the run frequency steady without adding fatigue.
days.title: Easy road run
days.workout: Warm-up: 10 min easy jog and drills. Main set: 45 min easy flat road run, smooth and relaxed from start to finish. Cool-down: 10 min easy jog/walk and light mobility. Target: RPE 2-3/10. Terrain/equipment: flat road only.

days.purpose: Support recovery while building aerobic consistency.
days.title: Easy aerobic bike
days.workout: Warm-up: 10 min easy spin. Main set: 45 min easy aerobic cycling, keeping the pressure light and the cadence smooth. Cool-down: 10 min easy spin plus hip and calf mobility. Target: RPE 2/10. Terrain/equipment: flat road or trainer.

days.purpose: Consolidate the early build and keep fatigue from stacking.
days.title: Rest day
days.workout: Warm-up: 10 min gentle walk and mobility. Main set: full rest from training; keep activity light and optional. Cool-down: 5-10 min stretching and breathing. Target: RPE 1/10. Terrain/equipment: off feet, no structured session.

days.purpose: Extend continuous aerobic work while staying far from hard intensity.
days.title: Controlled aerobic road run
days.workout: Warm-up: 10 min easy jog plus drills. Main set: 50 min controlled aerobic road run, slightly firmer than easy but still conversational. Cool-down: 10 min easy jog/walk and mobility. Target: RPE 3-4/10. Terrain/equipment: flat road only.

days.purpose: Repeat the long trail pattern and reinforce climbing economy.
days.target_intensity: RPE 3/10, easy aerobic with hiking on climbs
days.title: Long trail run
days.workout: Warm-up: 15 min easy jog on gentle terrain and a few drills. Main set: 90 min easy trail run with smooth uphill hiking and conservative downhill effort. Cool-down: 15 min easy jog/walk and mobility. Target: RPE 3/10. Terrain/equipment: weekend trail with moderate climbing.

days.purpose: Maintain trail frequency before the only quality session in the block.
days.title: Easy trail run
days.workout: Warm-up: 10 min easy jog plus mobility. Main set: 65 min easy trail run on rolling weekend terrain, keeping effort conversational and descents smooth. Cool-down: 10 min walk/jog and mobility. Target: RPE 2-3/10. Terrain/equipment: weekend trail.

days.purpose: Keep the aerobic rhythm going before quality later in the week.
days.title: Easy road run
days.workout: Warm-up: 10 min easy jog and drills. Main set: 40 min easy flat road run, relaxed and efficient. Cool-down: 10 min easy jog/walk and mobility. Target: RPE 2-3/10. Terrain/equipment: flat road only.

days.purpose: Absorb the load before the tempo day and protect the legs.
days.title: Easy aerobic bike
days.workout: Warm-up: 10 min easy spin. Main set: 40 min easy aerobic ride with smooth cadence and low resistance. Cool-down: 10 min easy spin and mobility. Target: RPE 2/10. Terrain/equipment: flat road or trainer.

days.purpose: Touch controlled quality without stacking intensity.
days.title: Controlled tempo road run
days.workout: Warm-up: 15 min easy jog plus drills and a few relaxed pickups. Main set: 3x8 min at controlled tempo effort with 3 min easy jog between reps, then 10 min steady aerobic running. Cool-down: 20 min easy jog/walk and mobility. Target: RPE 6/10. Terrain/equipment: flat road only. Modification: if fatigue is still high, convert this to a 60 min easy road run and skip the tempo reps.

days.purpose: Recover from tempo and keep the 7-day hard count at one.
days.title: Rest day
days.workout: Warm-up: 5-10 min walk and mobility. Main set: full rest from training; optional gentle walk only. Cool-down: 5-10 min stretching and breathing. Target: RPE 1/10. Terrain/equipment: off feet, no structured session.

days.purpose: Rebuild volume after the quality day without adding stress.
days.title: Easy road run
days.workout: Warm-up: 10 min easy jog and mobility. Main set: 47 min easy road run, smooth and conversational on flat terrain. Cool-down: 10 min easy jog/walk and mobility. Target: RPE 2-3/10. Terrain/equipment: flat road only.

days.purpose: Maintain long-run trail specificity and endurance while staying aerobic.
days.target_intensity: RPE 3/10, easy aerobic with hiking on climbs
days.title: Long trail run
days.workout: Warm-up: 15 min easy jog plus drills. Main set: 100 min easy trail run on rolling terrain, hiking steep sections and keeping the pace patient. Cool-down: 15 min easy jog/walk and mobility. Target: RPE 3/10. Terrain/equipment: weekend trail with moderate climbing.

days.purpose: Start the recovery/consolidation week and shed accumulated fatigue.
days.title: Rest day
days.workout: Warm-up: 10 min gentle walk and mobility. Main set: full rest from training; keep activity light and optional. Cool-down: 5-10 min breathing and stretching. Target: RPE 1/10. Terrain/equipment: off feet, no structured session.

days.purpose: Keep movement going while holding the week in a recovery emphasis.
days.title: Easy aerobic bike
days.workout: Warm-up: 10 min easy spin. Main set: 35 min easy aerobic cycling with relaxed cadence and no forceful efforts. Cool-down: 10 min easy spin and mobility. Target: RPE 2/10. Terrain/equipment: flat road or trainer.

days.purpose: Maintain run rhythm without adding intensity in the recovery week.
days.title: Easy road run
days.workout: Warm-up: 10 min easy jog plus drills. Main set: 40 min easy flat road run, relaxed and controlled. Cool-down: 10 min easy jog/walk and mobility. Target: RPE 2-3/10. Terrain/equipment: flat road only.

days.purpose: Add a second rest day in the consolidation week so the load drops meaningfully.
days.title: Rest day
days.workout: Warm-up: 5-10 min walk and mobility. Main set: full rest from training. Cool-down: 5-10 min stretching and breathing. Target: RPE 1/10. Terrain/equipment: off feet, no structured session.

days.purpose: Keep aerobic maintenance in the recovery week without adding strain.
days.title: Controlled aerobic road run
days.workout: Warm-up: 10 min easy jog plus drills. Main set: 45 min controlled aerobic road run, steady but still conversational. Cool-down: 10 min easy jog/walk and mobility. Target: RPE 3-4/10. Terrain/equipment: flat road only.

days.purpose: Finish the consolidation week with low-impact aerobic work.
days.title: Easy aerobic bike
days.workout: Warm-up: 10 min easy spin. Main set: 30 min easy aerobic cycling with low resistance and smooth cadence. Cool-down: 10 min easy spin and mobility. Target: RPE 2/10. Terrain/equipment: flat road or trainer.

days.purpose: Retain trail contact while the block stays in recovery/consolidation mode.
days.title: Easy trail run
days.workout: Warm-up: 15 min easy jog plus mobility on gentle terrain. Main set: 80 min easy trail run on rolling weekend terrain, keeping the effort comfortable and the descents controlled. Cool-down: 15 min easy jog/walk and mobility. Target: RPE 2-3/10. Terrain/equipment: weekend trail with moderate climbing.

actions: Aim for a consistent 8+ hour sleep opportunity nightly and keep the wind-down routine stable.
Keep weekday sessions flat and easy; do not add hills or extra quality while fatigue is still present.
Fuel any session longer than 75 minutes with normal carbohydrate intake before and after, and recover with a mixed meal within 1-2 hours.
Hydrate on bike and run days, and keep post-session mobility to 10-15 minutes rather than adding heavy strength work this block.

risks.message: Recent training load is elevated versus baseline and recovery signals are still muted, so unplanned intensity could compound fatigue.
Only one hard session is scheduled in any 7-day window; adding extra tempo, intervals, or hill work would break the guardrails.
Weekend-only trail access compresses specificity, so weekday road and bike work must stay truly easy and flat.
HRV is unavailable in the recent telemetry, which lowers confidence in fine-grained recovery calls.

snapshot.notes: Recent 7 days were concentrated: 7 activities (3 runs, 3 rides, 1 trail run), 110.3 km, 8.05 h moving time, and 1578 m gain. The biggest single stressor was the 2026-03-21 trail run with 1393 m of climbing. HRV is unavailable in the recent telemetry, so recovery interpretation leans on sleep and resting HR. Historical trail-running capacity is strong, but the current block is anchored to present fatigue, not past peaks.
